Elder Spiritist's Boots  
 

Lore Item
Slot: FEET
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 8
Effect: Levitate (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 6.0) at Level 45
WT: 6.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: DRU
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Elder Spiritist's Boots
Lore Item
Slot: FEET
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 8
Effect: Levitate (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 6.0) at Level 45
WT: 6.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: DRU
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Chain
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):10, 50, 10
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:330 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:668
Submitted By:Cadinos
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-19 10:12:45
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 975pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Very Rare
Level to Attain: 50
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Howling Stones (Charasis)
NPC Name
a mortiferous golem
Mortiferous Protector


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Howling Stones (Charasis)

Must equip !

# Jul 06 2007 at 5:05 AM Rating: Decent
Ikkysar
Scholar
73 posts
I may be completely wrong... but I think must equip means that you have to equip them to be able to right click them. Once you're buffed you can just take them off again.
____________________________
Claxin Milactium ~ 32 Paladin ~ Terris Thule (RIP)
Frayleaf Cloudrunner ~ 90 Bard ~ The Rathe
Intel ~ 60 Enchanter ~ Fippy Darkpaw

Charasis

# Mar 20 2003 at 11:11 PM Rating: Decent

112 posts
These drop in Charasis from a Mortiferious Protector. Right click levitate.
▲ Page top
what lvl?
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2002 at 3:00 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591781472337
3 posts
What is the minimum lvl to use the effect on the ES boots?
▲ Page top
Level 45 is the minimumn level for Kunark gear such as this
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2002 at 3:14 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592252462989
View User Forum
Sage
211 posts
Hello dear

Level 45 is the min level. Note when this came out level 45 and this effect were very "high level" gear. It is most often sought after pre SOE spell these days, and is happily sought after by the save the bat faction.
▲ Page top
Complete Crap!
QuoteReply
# Nov 03 2002 at 4:47 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760222876
15 posts
Just wasted 1k on these for Dru, and was horribly disapointed. Guy told me they did in fact not use a bat wing. Wish I would have checked this place first.

As not to just sound like a ranter, no they do not work outside of inventory. At least they didn't for me.

Disappointing
QuoteReply
# Feb 26 2002 at 5:07 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591770176339
View User Forum
71 posts
I have these boots and I have to say, I never wear them. I wear ES leggings and if worn with these boots, it looks so awful. Too much dark green removes any distinction. So I continue to wear my eBoots to add some color to my ES armor attire. I hardly use levitation anyways.

263 posts
I keep a set of gear that I use for hunting/grouping/raiding, and another set that I wear when I'm doing anything else. The battle gear looks awful. The Everyday Wear looks spiffy. No one said that how we look is the most important thing, but it can be a factor.

batwings
QuoteReply
# Jan 29 2002 at 5:06 PM Rating: Decent
-REDACTED-
126 posts
There is an upgrade to the druid spell Levitate that came out with luclin... it is Levitation that requires no batwing and uses 60mana to cast. it is a lvl 51 spell i believe....

It would make sense, and totally contradict the post below from Verant representative, to make these boots, only usable by a lvl 45 druid mind you, use the same spell that requires no bat wing. other wise.. doubt many will keep cept for dressing up for special occasions.

There is also an Enduring breath spell at lvl 51 that requires no fish scales...

I have yet to get these but they are on my 'want' list.

▲ Page top
RE: batwings
QuoteReply
# Nov 08 2002 at 1:02 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591776013048
View User Forum
263 posts
Uh, bub, what would an ogre warrior want with those spells ;)

Many of us carry around a stack of batwings for so long that it wouldn't be much of an inconvenience to continue to do so.
▲ Page top
Yes Batwings
QuoteReply
# Dec 03 2001 at 9:39 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252087103
1 post
Looted a pair this weekend and it uses bat wings. Not trading my GEBs for these. Maybe if you have no wings, it might still go off, but if you have wings, they definately get used.
▲ Page top
RE: Yes Batwings
QuoteReply
# Aug 30 2002 at 1:39 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591777329534
Scholar
24 posts
You definately have to have batwings to make these work. I'm not sure I would have bought them if I had known that aheaed of time, but I got them for 350 plat so I figured why not? Saves a spell slot at the very least.
▲ Page top
No Batwings
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 4:32 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246387256
4 posts
Greetings.

From what I have seen on my friends character, and wifes character, you no longer need batwings to use the effect of this spell on the boots.

That's to solve some *I wonders* below.

Thulgar Spiritwolf
Brell Serilis
▲ Page top
Booties
QuoteReply
# May 20 2001 at 8:39 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The ES Booties drop off of Mortiferious Protector (a named Golem) in HS. In the same room, the zone boss, Drusella Sathir (necro), also spawns - along with her guards. We did the room using one group consisting of: one SK (puller), one warrior (chain puller), one ranger, one chanter, one cleric, and one druid. The ranger and the cleric were the lowest levels in the group at 54. (No - we didn't have a rogue). The key to our success was the pullers who broght single to the group. The boots seem to be common - got 2 pair back-to-back.

Were the booties worth the effort? Other than looks, probably not. If batwings were not needed to use the effect, then I would say "yes", till then...

Khardan, 60th Druid
Guardians of Honor
Test Server
▲ Page top
da boots
QuoteReply
# May 16 2001 at 4:08 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758926994
4 posts
These are kinda nice to have. Just have in open inventory slot so you can swap the gebs and click. It frees up another spell slot. Other than that no biggie. I like to have 8 10 slotters on me, but now I must make sacrifices.
▲ Page top
Lvl 45
QuoteReply
# May 04 2001 at 7:43 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The not sufficient lvl indicates that at 19 you cant proc the effect..which will remain the case untill your wife hits 45 most likley as thats the lvl for all the ES gear i know.

As for requiring a wing, OMG what a waist of time..at lvl 45 the mana to cast levitate is insignificant..same as the crap snare on the ES guantlets.
▲ Page top
RE: Lvl 45
QuoteReply
# Nov 08 2002 at 1:04 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591776013048
View User Forum
263 posts
Saves spell slots though, bub. It's much easier to do a quick switch of boots, click, then switch back than to swap out spells, especially with the customizable UI that you didn't have back then.
▲ Page top
This is where they drop....
QuoteReply
# Mar 20 2001 at 3:27 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591757432373
6 posts
The boots are found in the east wing of Howling Stones. The mob is called a Mortiferious Protector. He's in the room with Drusella Sathir (who drops the NoS and is on like an 8 hour timer). Pretty nasty mob who hits in the 160 range. We take the room with a group of 6...usually 55+. I think the last time we went only the cleric was under 55. The ench was 55 and the rest of us 57+.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Mar 22 2001 at 2:47 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous

batwings..
QuoteReply
# Mar 09 2001 at 4:00 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750069622
Scholar
88 posts
They mwntioned in the recent patches about possibly changing the ES boots so you do not need batwings to use them, however it hasnt been done yet. It is a right clickable effect of levitate that requires the use of batwings. (with the low mana cost of levitate anyway why even bother with these except for the status?)
▲ Page top
ES Boots...
QuoteReply
# Sep 21 2001 at 1:58 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1593965874600
View User Forum
Sage
268 posts
I emailed Verent asking that question.

====================
MY QUESTION
====================

"Currently these boots require a component (Bat Wing) just like normal Leviate spells do.

As the item stands now, it is not very worthwhile to use as the normal leviatate spells that Druids use costs minimal mana.

Is there any plans to change this item in the future to not use a component?

Thank you in advance if you address this issue,
-Miriamele . Brell Serilis"

====================
THEIR RESPONSE
====================

The general rule is that items that have spells that normally use components will also require components.

This is not likely to change.


Alan VanCouvering
Community Relations Manager, EverQuest
Verant Interactive
▲ Page top
Info
QuoteReply
# Feb 02 2001 at 11:14 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
From the information i have gathered, the ES boots drop in the east wing of Charsis(howling stones). Found it kinda lame that in order to use the levitate effect, u still require batwings.
